use_cases.AC1: Initial Page State — page loads with correct heading, instructional text, and hot spot element
use_cases.AC2: Right-Click Alert Behavior — right-click inside hot spot triggers JavaScript alert dialog
use_cases.AC3: Negative Cases and Boundary Conditions — verify alert only triggers under correct conditions
use_cases.AC4: Edge Cases and Additional Validation — border clicks and exact message validation

tests.title: Verify page loads with hot spot box visible and properly styled
Right-click inside hot spot triggers alert with exact message
Dismissing the alert leaves page in normal state with no navigation
Right-clicking hot spot repeatedly triggers alert each time (repeatable behavior)
Right-clicking outside the hot spot does NOT trigger the alert
URL remains unchanged throughout all interactions
Left-clicking inside the hot spot does NOT trigger the alert
Page content remains intact after multiple alert dismiss cycles
Right-click on the edge/border of the hot spot triggers the alert
Alert message is exact and case-sensitive

defects.title: Dialog handler race condition causes failure on repeated right-click alert cycles
defects.steps: Navigate to the context menu page, right-click the hot spot 5 times in succession, dismissing each alert.
defects.expected: Each right-click triggers a new alert that can be accepted independently, and page content remains intact after all cycles.
defects.actual: Test fails with 'Cannot accept dialog which is already handled!' — the dialog is auto-dismissed before the explicit accept call, causing a race condition in the handler on repeated alert cycles.
